l1: Are users unable to authenticate after Windows Defender Credential Guard was enabled?
Is the authentication failure happening with single sign-on (SSO) or remote desktops?
Are the endpoints running enterprise, education, or professional editions of Windows?
l2: Is Virtualization-Based Security (VBS) running and healthy on the target client?
Are legacy NTLMv1 or un-delegated Kerberos requests causing the authentication drop?
Does the client hardware have an active TPM (Trusted Platform Module) enabled?

l1: Run 'msinfo32.exe' and check the bottom of the System Summary for VBS status.
Check the Security event log for Event ID 4624 (Logon Type 3/NLA failures).
Review the Microsoft-Windows-DeviceGuard event log for active operational alerts.
l2: Run 'Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_DeviceGuard' via PowerShell to query runtime status.
Check the LSA event logs for Event IDs 123 or 124 confirming isolated LSA launch status.
Verify TPM provisioning status by executing 'tpm.msc' on the client device.

l1: Disable Credential Guard via Group Policy to test if the authentication problem clears.
Ensure the targeted network application supports modern Kerberos authentication.
Enable Restricted Admin mode for Remote Desktop connections to work with Credential Guard.
l2: Enable Secure Boot and Virtualization extensions (Intel VT-x/AMD-V) in the device BIOS.
Configure Kerberos constrained delegation if third-party backend services fail.
Clear the TPM chip from within the OS if security endorsement keys become corrupted.

l1: Export the full DeviceGuard operational event log channel to a text file.
Generate an html report using the Device Guard and Credential Guard hardware readiness script.
Collect text outputs detailing system metrics from the msinfo32 command utility.
l2: Gather hardware virtualization diagnostics using the core Microsoft hardware readiness tool.
Capture system security auditing dumps focusing on NTLM block events.

vbs_not_running.title: Remediating Credential Guard Activation Failures Due to Missing VBS Infrastructure

steps.expect: Output returns blank or does not list value '1' (Credential Guard)
State must be 'Enabled'; if Disabled, the primary hypervisor layer is missing
Feature state changes to enabled; requires a subsequent physical system reboot
Output explicitly lists '1', indicating Windows Defender Credential Guard is active
